title: cistus × escartianus nothosubsp. navarroi
intro: Cistus × escartianus nothosubsp. navarroi, commonly known as the Navarro cistus, is a hybrid plant species within the Cistus genus, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers and compact growth habit. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cistus × escartianus nothosubsp. navarroi include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
